title: Club
summary: A simple bludgeoning weapon carved from a sturdy piece of wood.
content:
## Club

A simple melee weapon fashioned from a heavy piece of wood.

**Damage:** 1d4 bludgeoning
**Properties:** Light
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 1 sp

### Properties

**Light.** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

title: Dagger
summary: A small, easily concealed blade useful for both melee and ranged attacks.
content:
## Dagger

A simple, versatile blade favored by rogues and spellcasters alike.

**Damage:** 1d4 piercing
**Properties:** Finesse, Light, Thrown (range 20/60)
**Weight:** 1 lb.
**Cost:** 2 gp

### Properties

**Finesse.** When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

**Light.** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

**Thrown.** You can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. The normal range is 20 feet and the long range is 60 feet.

title: Greatclub
summary: A large, heavy club requiring two hands to wield effectively.
content:
## Greatclub

A large, heavy wooden club that requires two hands to swing.

**Damage:** 1d8 bludgeoning
**Properties:** Two-Handed
**Weight:** 10 lb.
**Cost:** 2 sp

### Properties

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Handaxe
summary: A small, light axe that can be wielded in melee or thrown at enemies.
content:
## Handaxe

A compact axe designed for one-handed use and throwing.

**Damage:** 1d6 slashing
**Properties:** Light, Thrown (range 20/60)
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 5 gp

### Properties

**Light.** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

**Thrown.** You can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. The normal range is 20 feet and the long range is 60 feet.

title: Javelin
summary: A light spear designed primarily for throwing but usable in melee.
content:
## Javelin

A light throwing spear commonly carried by warriors as a ranged option.

**Damage:** 1d6 piercing
**Properties:** Thrown (range 30/120)
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 5 sp

### Properties

**Thrown.** You can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. The normal range is 30 feet and the long range is 120 feet.

title: Light Hammer
summary: A small hammer that can be used in melee combat or thrown.
content:
## Light Hammer

A small, balanced hammer suitable for both melee and ranged combat.

**Damage:** 1d4 bludgeoning
**Properties:** Light, Thrown (range 20/60)
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 2 gp

### Properties

**Light.** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

**Thrown.** You can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. The normal range is 20 feet and the long range is 60 feet.

title: Mace
summary: A heavy-headed weapon on a shaft, favored by clerics and warriors.
content:
## Mace

A simple bludgeoning weapon with a heavy metal head, commonly carried by clerics.

**Damage:** 1d6 bludgeoning
**Properties:** None
**Weight:** 4 lb.
**Cost:** 5 gp

title: Quarterstaff
summary: A versatile wooden staff used by monks, wizards, and travelers alike.
content:
## Quarterstaff

A sturdy wooden pole used as a walking stick and weapon. Popular with monks and spellcasters.

**Damage:** 1d6 bludgeoning
**Properties:** Versatile (1d8)
**Weight:** 4 lb.
**Cost:** 2 sp

### Properties

**Versatile.** This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses (1d8) is for when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

title: Sickle
summary: A curved blade originally designed for harvesting grain, usable as a light weapon.
content:
## Sickle

A curved blade on a short handle, originally an agricultural tool repurposed as a weapon.

**Damage:** 1d4 slashing
**Properties:** Light
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 1 gp

### Properties

**Light.** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

title: Spear
summary: A versatile polearm that can be wielded one-handed, two-handed, or thrown.
content:
## Spear

One of the oldest and most common weapons, effective in melee and at range.

**Damage:** 1d6 piercing
**Properties:** Thrown (range 20/60), Versatile (1d8)
**Weight:** 3 lb.
**Cost:** 1 gp

### Properties

**Thrown.** You can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. The normal range is 20 feet and the long range is 60 feet.

**Versatile.** This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses (1d8) is for when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

title: Light Crossbow
summary: A mechanical ranged weapon that fires bolts, requiring two hands and loading time.
content:
## Light Crossbow

A compact crossbow that launches bolts with mechanical force.

**Damage:** 1d8 piercing
**Properties:** Ammunition (range 80/320), Loading, Two-Handed
**Weight:** 5 lb.
**Cost:** 25 gp

### Properties

**Ammunition.** You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. The normal range is 80 feet and the long range is 320 feet. You need a free hand to load the weapon.

**Loading.** Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Dart
summary: A small, pointed missile designed for throwing at range.
content:
## Dart

A small, aerodynamic missile thrown by hand.

**Damage:** 1d4 piercing
**Properties:** Finesse, Thrown (range 20/60)
**Weight:** 1/4 lb.
**Cost:** 5 cp

### Properties

**Finesse.** When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls.

**Thrown.** You can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. The normal range is 20 feet and the long range is 60 feet.

title: Shortbow
summary: A small bow favored for its portability and ease of use.
content:
## Shortbow

A compact bow commonly used by scouts and lightly armed warriors.

**Damage:** 1d6 piercing
**Properties:** Ammunition (range 80/320), Two-Handed
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 25 gp

### Properties

**Ammunition.** You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. The normal range is 80 feet and the long range is 320 feet.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Sling
summary: A simple ranged weapon that hurls stones or bullets with surprising force.
content:
## Sling

A pouch on a cord used to hurl stones or lead bullets at enemies.

**Damage:** 1d4 bludgeoning
**Properties:** Ammunition (range 30/120)
**Weight:** --
**Cost:** 1 sp

### Properties

**Ammunition.** You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. The normal range is 30 feet and the long range is 120 feet.

title: Battleaxe
summary: A heavy axe that can be wielded with one or two hands for devastating strikes.
content:
## Battleaxe

A sturdy axe designed for combat, effective with one or two hands.

**Damage:** 1d8 slashing
**Properties:** Versatile (1d10)
**Weight:** 4 lb.
**Cost:** 10 gp

### Properties

**Versatile.** This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses (1d10) is for when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

title: Flail
summary: A weighted head on a chain attached to a handle, delivering powerful bludgeoning blows.
content:
## Flail

A spiked metal ball attached to a handle by a chain, delivering crushing strikes.

**Damage:** 1d8 bludgeoning
**Properties:** None
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 10 gp

title: Glaive
summary: A long polearm with a curved blade, granting extended reach in combat.
content:
## Glaive

A single-edged blade mounted on the end of a long pole, granting superior reach.

**Damage:** 1d10 slashing
**Properties:** Heavy, Reach, Two-Handed
**Weight:** 6 lb.
**Cost:** 20 gp

### Properties

**Heavy.** Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons.

**Reach.** This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Greataxe
summary: A massive two-handed axe favored by barbarians for its brutal damage.
content:
## Greataxe

An enormous axe with a broad blade, favored by barbarians for its raw damage output.

**Damage:** 1d12 slashing
**Properties:** Heavy, Two-Handed
**Weight:** 7 lb.
**Cost:** 30 gp

### Properties

**Heavy.** Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Greatsword
summary: A large two-handed sword dealing heavy slashing damage with consistent output.
content:
## Greatsword

A massive sword requiring two hands, favored by fighters for its consistent high damage.

**Damage:** 2d6 slashing
**Properties:** Heavy, Two-Handed
**Weight:** 6 lb.
**Cost:** 50 gp

### Properties

**Heavy.** Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Halberd
summary: A polearm combining an axe blade and spike, offering reach and heavy damage.
content:
## Halberd

A polearm featuring an axe blade topped with a spike, combining reach with devastating power.

**Damage:** 1d10 slashing
**Properties:** Heavy, Reach, Two-Handed
**Weight:** 6 lb.
**Cost:** 20 gp

### Properties

**Heavy.** Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons.

**Reach.** This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Lance
summary: A long weapon with reach, designed for mounted combat but usable on foot with disadvantage at close range.
content:
## Lance

A long weapon designed for mounted charges, with extended reach.

**Damage:** 1d12 piercing
**Properties:** Reach, Special
**Weight:** 6 lb.
**Cost:** 10 gp

### Properties

**Reach.** This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it.

**Special.** You have disadvantage when you use a lance to attack a target within 5 feet of you. Also, a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren't mounted.

title: Longsword
summary: A classic versatile blade, the quintessential martial weapon for knights and warriors.
content:
## Longsword

The quintessential martial weapon, a balanced blade effective in one or two hands.

**Damage:** 1d8 slashing
**Properties:** Versatile (1d10)
**Weight:** 3 lb.
**Cost:** 15 gp

### Properties

**Versatile.** This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses (1d10) is for when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

title: Maul
summary: A massive two-handed hammer dealing devastating bludgeoning damage.
content:
## Maul

An enormous hammer designed to crush armor and bone alike.

**Damage:** 2d6 bludgeoning
**Properties:** Heavy, Two-Handed
**Weight:** 10 lb.
**Cost:** 10 gp

### Properties

**Heavy.** Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Morningstar
summary: A spiked mace that deals piercing damage with its heavy, studded head.
content:
## Morningstar

A heavy club or mace with a spiked head, delivering vicious piercing blows.

**Damage:** 1d8 piercing
**Properties:** None
**Weight:** 4 lb.
**Cost:** 15 gp

title: Pike
summary: A very long polearm with a pointed tip, offering superior reach.
content:
## Pike

An extremely long spear designed for formation combat, granting superior reach.

**Damage:** 1d10 piercing
**Properties:** Heavy, Reach, Two-Handed
**Weight:** 18 lb.
**Cost:** 5 gp

### Properties

**Heavy.** Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons.

**Reach.** This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Rapier
summary: A thin, pointed blade ideal for dexterous fighters who favor precision over brute force.
content:
## Rapier

A slender, sharply pointed sword optimized for thrusting attacks.

**Damage:** 1d8 piercing
**Properties:** Finesse
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 25 gp

### Properties

**Finesse.** When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

title: Scimitar
summary: A curved, single-edged blade that combines finesse with the light property for dual-wielding.
content:
## Scimitar

A curved sword with a single cutting edge, well-suited for quick, sweeping strikes.

**Damage:** 1d6 slashing
**Properties:** Finesse, Light
**Weight:** 3 lb.
**Cost:** 25 gp

### Properties

**Finesse.** When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls.

**Light.** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

title: Shortsword
summary: A short, light blade with finesse, popular with rogues for dual-wielding and sneak attacks.
content:
## Shortsword

A quick, light blade favored by rogues and dual-wielders.

**Damage:** 1d6 piercing
**Properties:** Finesse, Light
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 10 gp

### Properties

**Finesse.** When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls.

**Light.** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

title: Trident
summary: A three-pronged spear that can be used in melee or thrown, with versatile grip.
content:
## Trident

A three-pronged spear effective in both melee and ranged combat.

**Damage:** 1d6 piercing
**Properties:** Thrown (range 20/60), Versatile (1d8)
**Weight:** 4 lb.
**Cost:** 5 gp

### Properties

**Thrown.** You can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. The normal range is 20 feet and the long range is 60 feet.

**Versatile.** This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses (1d8) is for when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

title: War Pick
summary: A military pick designed to punch through armor with its pointed head.
content:
## War Pick

A military pick with a pointed head designed to penetrate armor.

**Damage:** 1d8 piercing
**Properties:** None
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 5 gp

title: Warhammer
summary: A versatile hammer favored by dwarves and paladins, effective one- or two-handed.
content:
## Warhammer

A sturdy hammer designed for battle, equally effective in one or two hands.

**Damage:** 1d8 bludgeoning
**Properties:** Versatile (1d10)
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 15 gp

### Properties

**Versatile.** This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses (1d10) is for when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

title: Whip
summary: A flexible weapon with reach and finesse, dealing modest slashing damage.
content:
## Whip

A braided leather weapon that strikes from a distance with surprising precision.

**Damage:** 1d4 slashing
**Properties:** Finesse, Reach
**Weight:** 3 lb.
**Cost:** 2 gp

### Properties

**Finesse.** When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls.

**Reach.** This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks.

title: Blowgun
summary: A tube that fires tiny needles, often used for delivering poison at range.
content:
## Blowgun

A slender tube used to fire tiny needles, often coated with poison.

**Damage:** 1 piercing
**Properties:** Ammunition (range 25/100), Loading
**Weight:** 1 lb.
**Cost:** 10 gp

### Properties

**Ammunition.** You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. The normal range is 25 feet and the long range is 100 feet.

**Loading.** Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

title: Hand Crossbow
summary: A small crossbow fired with one hand, popular with rogues using the Crossbow Expert feat.
content:
## Hand Crossbow

A compact crossbow designed to be fired with one hand.

**Damage:** 1d6 piercing
**Properties:** Ammunition (range 30/120), Light, Loading
**Weight:** 3 lb.
**Cost:** 75 gp

### Properties

**Ammunition.** You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. The normal range is 30 feet and the long range is 120 feet.

**Light.** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

**Loading.** Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

title: Heavy Crossbow
summary: A powerful two-handed crossbow with superior range and damage.
content:
## Heavy Crossbow

A large, powerful crossbow that delivers devastating bolts at long range.

**Damage:** 1d10 piercing
**Properties:** Ammunition (range 100/400), Heavy, Loading, Two-Handed
**Weight:** 18 lb.
**Cost:** 50 gp

### Properties

**Ammunition.** You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. The normal range is 100 feet and the long range is 400 feet.

**Heavy.** Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons.

**Loading.** Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Longbow
summary: A tall bow with superior range, the preferred weapon of elven archers and rangers.
content:
## Longbow

A tall bow nearly as long as the wielder, capable of striking targets at great distances.

**Damage:** 1d8 piercing
**Properties:** Ammunition (range 150/600), Heavy, Two-Handed
**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 50 gp

### Properties

**Ammunition.** You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. The normal range is 150 feet and the long range is 600 feet.

**Heavy.** Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons.

**Two-Handed.** This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

title: Net
summary: A thrown weapon that restrains creatures instead of dealing damage.
content:
## Net

A mesh of rope used to entangle and restrain creatures.

**Damage:** None
**Properties:** Special, Thrown (range 5/15)
**Weight:** 3 lb.
**Cost:** 1 gp

### Properties

**Special.** A Large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A net has no effect on creatures that are formless, or creatures that are Huge or larger. A creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the net (AC 10) also frees the creature without harming it, ending the effect and destroying the net.

**Thrown.** You can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. The normal range is 5 feet and the long range is 15 feet. When you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to attack with a net, you can make only one attack regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

title: Padded Armor
summary: Quilted layers of cloth and batting providing minimal protection with stealth penalty.
content:
## Padded Armor

Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.

**Armor Class:** 11 + Dex modifier
**Stealth:** Disadvantage
**Weight:** 8 lb.
**Cost:** 5 gp

Padded armor is the cheapest light armor available, but the bulk of its quilted layers imposes disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Leather Armor
summary: A breastpiece and shoulder protectors made of stiffened leather, a common choice for rogues.
content:
## Leather Armor

The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by boiling in oil.

**Armor Class:** 11 + Dex modifier
**Stealth:** No penalty
**Weight:** 10 lb.
**Cost:** 10 gp

Leather armor is a popular choice for characters who rely on stealth and agility, as it provides basic protection without hindering movement.

title: Studded Leather Armor
summary: Tough leather reinforced with metal rivets, the best light armor available.
content:
## Studded Leather Armor

Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.

**Armor Class:** 12 + Dex modifier
**Stealth:** No penalty
**Weight:** 13 lb.
**Cost:** 45 gp

Studded leather is the best light armor available, offering superior protection while still allowing full Dexterity bonus to AC and no stealth penalty.

title: Hide Armor
summary: Crude armor made from thick animal hides and pelts.
content:
## Hide Armor

This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts.

**Armor Class:** 12 + Dex modifier (max 2)
**Stealth:** No penalty
**Weight:** 12 lb.
**Cost:** 10 gp

Hide armor is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and others who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.

title: Chain Shirt
summary: Interlocking metal rings worn under clothing, offering decent protection.
content:
## Chain Shirt

Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather.

**Armor Class:** 13 + Dex modifier (max 2)
**Stealth:** No penalty
**Weight:** 20 lb.
**Cost:** 50 gp

The chain shirt provides good protection while remaining relatively quiet, making it a solid choice for characters who need moderate armor without stealth penalties.

title: Scale Mail
summary: Armor made of overlapping metal scales, offering good protection but hindering stealth.
content:
## Scale Mail

This armor consists of a coat and leggings of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish.

**Armor Class:** 14 + Dex modifier (max 2)
**Stealth:** Disadvantage
**Weight:** 45 lb.
**Cost:** 50 gp

Scale mail offers strong protection for its cost but the rigid, overlapping scales create noise that imposes disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Breastplate
summary: A fitted metal chest piece that protects vital organs without impeding movement.
content:
## Breastplate

This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.

**Armor Class:** 14 + Dex modifier (max 2)
**Stealth:** No penalty
**Weight:** 20 lb.
**Cost:** 400 gp

The breastplate is the best medium armor that does not impose stealth disadvantage, making it ideal for rangers, rogues multiclassing into martial classes, and other characters who balance protection with agility.

title: Half Plate
summary: Shaped metal plates covering most of the body, the best medium armor for raw AC.
content:
## Half Plate

Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves attached with leather straps.

**Armor Class:** 15 + Dex modifier (max 2)
**Stealth:** Disadvantage
**Weight:** 40 lb.
**Cost:** 750 gp

Half plate provides the highest AC of any medium armor, but the bulky plates impose disadvantage on Stealth checks.

title: Ring Mail
summary: Heavy armor made of leather with heavy metal rings sewn on. Provides a flat AC of 14 — DEX modifier is NOT added.
content:
## Ring Mail

This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes.

**Armor Class:** 14
**Stealth:** Disadvantage
**Weight:** 40 lb.
**Cost:** 30 gp

Ring mail is inferior to chain mail and is usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.

title: Chain Mail
summary: Interlocking metal rings forming a full suit of armor, requiring Strength 13.
content:
## Chain Mail

Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.

**Armor Class:** 16
**Strength Required:** 13
**Stealth:** Disadvantage
**Weight:** 55 lb.
**Cost:** 75 gp

Chain mail is the standard heavy armor for many starting fighters and paladins. If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.

title: Splint Armor
summary: Armor made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to leather, requiring Strength 15.
content:
## Splint Armor

This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.

**Armor Class:** 17
**Strength Required:** 15
**Stealth:** Disadvantage
**Weight:** 60 lb.
**Cost:** 200 gp

Splint armor provides excellent protection at a lower cost than plate. If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.

title: Plate Armor
summary: The finest heavy armor, consisting of shaped interlocking metal plates covering the entire body.
content:
## Plate Armor

Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.

**Armor Class:** 18
**Strength Required:** 15
**Stealth:** Disadvantage
**Weight:** 65 lb.
**Cost:** 1,500 gp

Plate armor provides the highest AC of any nonmagical armor. If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.

title: Shield
summary: A carried defensive item that grants +2 AC, requiring one free hand to use.
content:
## Shield

A shield is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your Armor Class by 2.

**Armor Class:** +2
**Weight:** 6 lb.
**Cost:** 10 gp

You can benefit from only one shield at a time. You must have proficiency with shields to use one effectively. Donning a shield takes 1 action, and doffing it also takes 1 action.

title: Backpack
summary: A leather pack with straps for carrying gear on your back, holding up to 30 pounds.
content:
## Backpack

A leather pack carried on the back, designed to hold gear for travel and adventuring.

**Capacity:** 1 cubic foot / 30 pounds of gear
**Weight:** 5 lb.
**Cost:** 2 gp

You can also strap items, such as a bedroll or a coil of rope, to the outside of a backpack.

title: Bedroll
summary: A portable sleeping mat and blanket for resting during travel.
content:
## Bedroll

A portable sleeping roll providing basic comfort during long rests in the wilderness.

**Weight:** 7 lb.
**Cost:** 1 gp

A bedroll provides minimal insulation and cushioning for sleeping on the ground.

title: Rope, Hempen (50 feet)
summary: A 50-foot length of hempen rope with 2 hit points that can be burst with a DC 17 Strength check.
content:
## Rope, Hempen (50 feet)

A 50-foot length of hempen rope, useful for climbing, binding, and countless other tasks.

**Weight:** 10 lb.
**Cost:** 1 gp

Rope has 2 hit points and can be burst with a DC 17 Strength check. It can be used for climbing, tying up prisoners, creating trip lines, and many other purposes.

title: Rations (1 day)
summary: Dry food suitable for extended travel, including jerky, dried fruit, and hardtack.
content:
## Rations (1 day)

Rations consist of dry foods suitable for extended travel, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts.

**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 5 sp

A character needs one pound of food per day and can make food last longer by subsisting on half rations, though this risks exhaustion.

title: Waterskin
summary: A leather pouch for carrying drinking water, holding up to 4 pints.
content:
## Waterskin

A supple leather pouch that holds up to 4 pints of liquid.

**Weight:** 5 lb. (full)
**Cost:** 2 sp

A character needs one gallon of water per day, or two gallons per day in hot weather.

title: Torch
summary: A wooden stick wrapped in oil-soaked cloth, providing bright light for 1 hour.
content:
## Torch

A torch burns for 1 hour, providing bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

**Weight:** 1 lb.
**Cost:** 1 cp

If you make a melee attack with a burning torch and hit, it deals 1 fire damage.

title: Tinderbox
summary: A small container with flint, fire steel, and tinder for starting fires.
content:
## Tinderbox

This small container holds flint, fire steel, and tinder (usually dry cloth soaked in a light oil) used to kindle a fire.

**Weight:** 1 lb.
**Cost:** 5 sp

Using a tinderbox to light a torch or anything else with abundant, exposed fuel takes an action. Lighting any other fire takes 1 minute.

title: Grappling Hook
summary: An iron hook with multiple prongs, used with rope for climbing.
content:
## Grappling Hook

A multi-pronged iron hook designed to be tied to a rope for climbing walls and other surfaces.

**Weight:** 4 lb.
**Cost:** 2 gp

When tied to the end of a rope, a grappling hook can be thrown to catch on ledges, window sills, or other protrusions.

title: Healer's Kit
summary: A leather pouch with bandages, salves, and splints with 10 uses for stabilizing creatures.
content:
## Healer's Kit

This kit is a leather pouch containing bandages, salves, and splints. The kit has ten uses.

**Uses:** 10
**Weight:** 3 lb.
**Cost:** 5 gp

As an action, you can expend one use of the kit to stabilize a creature that has 0 hit points, without needing to make a Wisdom (Medicine) check.

title: Piton
summary: An iron spike that can be hammered into rock or ice to anchor a rope.
content:
## Piton

An iron spike designed to be hammered into a crack or seam in rock, used as an anchor for ropes during climbing.

**Weight:** 1/4 lb.
**Cost:** 5 cp

Pitons are typically used in conjunction with rope and a hammer for climbing cliff faces, dungeon walls, and other vertical surfaces.

title: Crowbar
summary: An iron lever that grants advantage on Strength checks where leverage can be applied.
content:
## Crowbar

A flat iron bar with a curved end, used for prying open doors, crates, and other objects.

**Weight:** 5 lb.
**Cost:** 2 gp

Using a crowbar grants advantage to Strength checks where the crowbar's leverage can be applied.

title: Hammer
summary: A standard hammer useful for driving pitons and general construction tasks.
content:
## Hammer

A basic hammer for driving pitons, nailing boards, and other tasks.

**Weight:** 3 lb.
**Cost:** 1 gp

Commonly carried by adventurers for driving pitons into rock when climbing.

title: Holy Symbol
summary: A representation of a god or pantheon, used as a spellcasting focus by clerics and paladins.
content:
## Holy Symbol

A holy symbol is a representation of a god or pantheon. It might be an amulet depicting a symbol of a deity, the same symbol carefully engraved or inlaid on a shield, or a tiny box holding a fragment of a sacred relic.

**Forms:** Amulet, Emblem, or Reliquary
**Weight:** 1 lb. (varies by form)
**Cost:** 5 gp

A cleric or paladin can use a holy symbol as a spellcasting focus. To use the symbol in this way, the caster must hold it in hand, wear it visibly, or bear it on a shield.

title: Arcane Focus
summary: A special item (crystal, orb, rod, staff, or wand) used as a spellcasting focus by arcane casters.
content:
## Arcane Focus

An arcane focus is a special item designed to channel the power of arcane spells. A sorcerer, warlock, or wizard can use such an item as a spellcasting focus.

**Forms:** Crystal, Orb, Rod, Staff, or Wand
**Weight:** Varies (1-4 lb.)
**Cost:** 5-20 gp (varies by form)

An arcane focus can be used in place of material components for spells, as long as the components have no listed cost and are not consumed by the spell.

title: Component Pouch
summary: A small waterproof belt pouch containing all the material components for spellcasting.
content:
## Component Pouch

A small, watertight leather belt pouch that has compartments to hold all of the material components and other special items you need to cast your spells, except for those components that have a specific cost.

**Weight:** 2 lb.
**Cost:** 25 gp

A component pouch can be used in place of material components for spells, as long as the components have no listed cost and are not consumed by the spell. It serves as an alternative to an arcane or druidic focus.

title: Spellbook
summary: A leather-bound tome essential for wizards, containing 100 blank parchment pages.
content:
## Spellbook

Essential for wizards, this leather-bound tome has 100 blank vellum pages suitable for recording spells.

**Weight:** 3 lb.
**Cost:** 50 gp

A wizard's spellbook is a unique collection of spells, with its own decorative flourishes and margin notes. A wizard begins play with a spellbook containing six 1st-level wizard spells of the player's choice. Copying a spell into the spellbook takes 2 hours and 50 gp per spell level.

title: Thieves' Tools
summary: A set of lockpicks, a small file, pliers, and other tools for disarming traps and picking locks.
content:
## Thieves' Tools

This set of tools includes a small file, a set of lock picks, a small mirror mounted on a metal handle, a set of narrow-bladed scissors, and a pair of pliers.

**Weight:** 1 lb.
**Cost:** 25 gp

Proficiency with thieves' tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to disarm traps or open locks. Picking a lock typically requires a Dexterity check using thieves' tools.

title: Explorer's Pack
summary: A pre-assembled pack for wilderness exploration with essential adventuring supplies.
content:
## Explorer's Pack

A collection of essential items for wilderness exploration and dungeon delving.

**Cost:** 10 gp
**Weight:** 59 lb. (total)

### Contents
- Backpack
- Bedroll
- Mess kit
- Tinderbox
- 10 torches
- 10 days of rations
- Waterskin
- 50 feet of hempen rope

title: Dungeoneer's Pack
summary: A pre-assembled pack for dungeon exploration with pitons, crowbar, and other essentials.
content:
## Dungeoneer's Pack

A collection of items suited for exploring dungeons and underground environments.

**Cost:** 12 gp
**Weight:** 61.5 lb. (total)

### Contents
- Backpack
- Crowbar
- Hammer
- 10 pitons
- 10 torches
- Tinderbox
- 10 days of rations
- Waterskin
- 50 feet of hempen rope

title: Priest's Pack
summary: A pre-assembled pack for divine casters with holy items, candles, and basic supplies.
content:
## Priest's Pack

A collection of items suited for divine spellcasters and those on holy missions.

**Cost:** 19 gp
**Weight:** 24 lb. (total)

### Contents
- Backpack
- Blanket
- 10 candles
- Tinderbox
- Alms box
- 2 blocks of incense
- Censer
- Vestments
- 2 days of rations
- Waterskin

title: Scholar's Pack
summary: A pre-assembled pack for wizards and scholars with a book, ink, parchment, and supplies.
content:
## Scholar's Pack

A collection of items suited for academic pursuits and arcane research.

**Cost:** 40 gp
**Weight:** 10 lb. (total)

### Contents
- Backpack
- Book of lore
- Bottle of ink
- Ink pen
- 10 sheets of parchment
- Little bag of sand
- Small knife

title: Antitoxin
summary: A vial of alchemically prepared liquid that grants advantage against poison for 1 hour.
content:
## Antitoxin

A creature that drinks this vial of liquid gains **advantage on saving throws against poison** for 1 hour. It confers no benefit to undead or constructs.

**Cost:** 50 gp
**Weight:** — (negligible)
**Not magical** — works by alchemical means.

title: Holy Water (flask)
summary: A flask of water blessed by a cleric or paladin that burns fiends and undead.
content:
## Holy Water

As an action, splash onto a creature within 5 feet or throw up to 20 feet. Make a ranged attack (improvised weapon).

If the target is a **fiend or undead**, it takes **2d6 radiant damage**.

A cleric or paladin may create holy water (1 hour ritual, 25 gp of powdered silver).

**Cost:** 25 gp
**Weight:** 1 lb

title: Alchemist's Fire (flask)
summary: A sticky, flammable fluid that ignites on contact with air.
content:
## Alchemist's Fire

This sticky fluid ignites when exposed to air. Throw up to 20 feet as an action (improvised weapon ranged attack).

On a hit, the target takes **1d4 fire damage** at the start of each of its turns. A creature can end this with a **DC 10 Dexterity check** (action).

**Cost:** 50 gp
**Weight:** 1 lb

title: Acid (vial)
summary: A vial of corrosive acid that deals 2d6 acid damage when thrown.
content:
## Acid

As an action, splash onto a creature within 5 feet or throw up to 20 feet. Make a ranged attack (improvised weapon).

- **Hit:** 2d6 acid damage

**Cost:** 25 gp
**Weight:** 1 lb

title: Lantern, Bullseye
summary: A hooded lantern that casts bright light in a 60-foot cone.
content:
## Bullseye Lantern

Casts **bright light in a 60-foot cone** and dim light for an additional 60 feet. Burns for **6 hours** on a flask of oil. Lower the hood (action) to reduce to dim light in 5-foot radius.

**Cost:** 10 gp
**Weight:** 2 lb

title: Lantern, Hooded
summary: A common lantern that casts bright light in a 30-foot radius.
content:
## Hooded Lantern

Casts **bright light in a 30-foot radius** and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Burns for **6 hours** on a flask of oil. Lower the hood (action) to reduce to dim light in 5-foot radius.

**Cost:** 5 gp
**Weight:** 2 lb

title: Oil (flask)
summary: Lamp oil that can fuel lanterns or be thrown as a fire hazard.
content:
## Oil

Splash onto a creature within 5 feet or throw up to 20 feet. On a hit, target is **covered in oil**. If it takes fire damage before the oil dries (1 minute), it takes an additional **5 fire damage**.

Can pour on ground to cover 10 sq ft area — ignites if lit (DC 10 DEX save, 5 fire damage, burns 2 rounds). Fuels a lantern for **6 hours**.

**Cost:** 1 sp
**Weight:** 1 lb

title: Potion of Healing
summary: The most common magical item — a red liquid that restores 2d4+2 hit points.
content:
## Potion of Healing

*Potion, common*

You regain **2d4 + 2 hit points** when you drink this potion. The red liquid glimmers when agitated. Drinking or administering takes one action.

**Cost:** 50 gp
**Weight:** 1/2 lb
**Rarity:** Common
